content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5602        322-3660           322-5901        322-6441              322-6444


April 5, 1983


Mr. Allen R. Briggs
Assistant City Attorney
300 North "D" Street
San Bernardino, CA 92418

Re: Our Advice No. A-83-075

Dear Mr. Briggs:

     Thank you for your request for advice on the provisions of
Government Code Section 84308.1/ You stated that all of the
members of the San Bernardino Common Council and the Mayor are
elected officials. The councilmembers and the Mayor also serve
as the Community Development Commission, which is the governing
body of San Bernardino's Redevelopment Agency. Your question is
whether the Council and Mayor, while sitting as the Community
Redevelopment Commission, are subject to the provisions of
Section 84308.

     The Commission's regulation, 2 California Administrative
Code Section 18438.1, which interprets, in part, Section
84308(d), provides that:

     (a) A "legislative body" while acting as a body
or committee thereof is excluded by Government Code
Section 84308(a), (b) and (c) if its entire membership
consists of elected officers directly elected by the
voters to serve on that body...

     (b) A legislative body is "acting as a body or a
committee thereof" when:

          (1) It acts in in its entirety as itself or
     as the ex-officio governing body of any district
     or other entity...


_______________________
1/ All statutory references made are to the Government
Code unless otherwise noted.

Mr. Allen R. Briggs
April 5, 1983
Page Two


Since the entire membership of the Common Council and the
Mayor are directly elected, this group while acting in its
entirety as the ex-officio governing body of the Redevelopment
Agency, is exempt from the coverage of Section 84308.

If you have any additional questions, please do not hesitate
to contact me at 916/322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel, Legal Division

JSM:km

CITY OF SAN BERNARDINO                300 NORTH "D" STREET, SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA 92418


RALPH H. PRINCE
CITY ATTORNEY                                 March 17, 1983


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street Building
P.O. Box 607
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:  Request for Opinion, Community Development
     Commission Members Entitled to "Elected
     Officials" Exemption

Gentlemen:

The Mayor and members of the Common Council of the City of
San Bernardino are elected officials, and the Mayor, though
chief executive of the City, also serves as presiding officer
at all meetings of the Common Council. He wields limited
veto power. The veto power may be exercised at and during
the meeting as to motions, and within five days as to
resolutions and ordinances, as to any motion or resolution
not having at least five favorable votes (of seven).

In addition, the Mayor and all members of the Common Council,
automatically, by resolution of the Mayor and Common Council,
serve as the Community Development Commission, the governing
body of the Redevelopment Agency of the City of San
Bernardino, created pursuant to Health and Safety Code
Section 34110. The Community Development Commission when
dealing with redevelopment matters has been determined to be
an arm of state government (Gibbs v. City of Napa [1976] 59
Cal.App.3d 148, 154; 120 Cal.Rptr. 362, 385). All members of
the Common Council and the Mayor serve on the Community
Development Commission solely and directly as a result of
their membership on the Common Council or as Mayor of the
City of San Bernardino.pursuant to the designation of the
Mayor and Common Council under the provisions of Health and
Safety Code Section 33200. (The City of San Bernardino is
the charter city for which the 1977 amendment affecting only

Fair Political Practices Commission
March 17, 1983
Page 2

San Bernardino County was enacted.) Each receives
compensation for service on the Community Development
Commission, over and above that compensation paid them as
members of the Common Council or as Mayor. They are not,
however, "elected" to the Community Development Commission,
but serve in that position solely as a result of their
serving as members of the Common Council or as Mayor.

Chapter 1049 of the Statutes of 1982, being Assembly Bill
1040, has added Section 84308 to the Government Code. That
statute places various restrictions upon solicitation of
contributions, or acceptance of contributions, or
participation in decisions where an interested party who has
made a contribution to certain officers may be involved.

We understand that the Fair Political Practices Commission
has issued an opinion or ruling, or adopted a rule,
purportedly establishing that members of a legislative body
are acting as a body or committee thereof when the body acts
in its entirety as the ex officio governing body of any
district or other entity. A copy of Attorney William Flory's
letter citing your Agency as authority is enclosed. No
actual "opinion" from the FPPC was attached to Mr. Flory's
letter.

This office has serious reservations as to whether the Mayor
and Common Council, when sitting as members of the governing
body of a state agency (the Community Development Commission
of the City of San Bernardino, governing body of the
Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Bernardino) are
exempt "while sitting as ex officio governing body of any
district or other entity."

It would seem that such would be the case at first glance.
However, since the sanctions involved are serious, the Mayor
of the City of San Bernardino has asked whether campaign
contributions of $250 or more to him require him to
disqualify himself from voting or from acting as a non-voting
Mayor or as the presiding officer and non-voting member of
the Community Development Commission. It is our initial
opinion that the members of the Common Council and the Mayor,
when sitting as members of the Redevelopment Agency's
governing body, are in effect serving as appointed officers
(by their own appointment), and such officials are not within

Fair Political Practices Commission
March 17, 1983
Page 3


the ambit of "those governmental bodies specified in
subdivision (d)". Under the provisions of Section 84308
Subparagraph A, "No elected or appointed officer, or
alternate, or candidate for office, who serve[s] on a quasi-
judicial board or commission, other than those governmental
bodies specified in subdivision (d), shall accept, solicit,
or direct a contribution of $250 or more, from any person or
his or her agent, who has an application for a license,
permit, or other entitlement for use pending before any body
of which the officer or alternate is a member and for three
months following the date a decision is rendered on the
application or from any person, or his or her agent, who
actively supports or opposes the application." The
Community Development Commission almost certainly qualifies
as a "quasi-judicial board or commission". We find nothing
which qualifies its members as exempt under Subdivision (d).
Thus, since the Board of a Redevelopment Agency does not
appear, under the statute, at least, to be exempted from that
requirement, we wonder why the FPPC would determine that when
members of a legislative body are sitting as a board of
another agency, they are exempt under the provisions of
subdivision (d).

We recognize the expertise of FPPC staff, and will certainly
be guided by your opinion or ruling.

We would appreciate any information or guidance you can
provide as to whether, in light of the express words of the
statute, the members of a Common Council of the City of San
Bernardino may properly serve on the Community Development
Commission (governing body of the Redevelopment Agency) and
consider matters relating to developers and others who have
made contributions within the past several months. Certainly
they are still "elected officials", but they do not appear to
us to be serving as "elected officials" when they are serving
on the Board of the Redevelopment Agency, to which they have
been appointed by their own action by virtue of their
position on the Common Council.

Fair Political Practices Commission
March 17, 1983
Page 4


Your consideration of this request will be greatly
appreciated.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        ALLEN R. BRIGGS
                                        Assistant City Attorney

ARB:lr

Enclosure

REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY
OF THE CITY OF SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA

GARY VAN OSDEL
EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR

RT. A. CASTANEDA
STEPHEN MARKS
JACK REILLY
JACK SRICKLER
JACK STRICKLER
RALPH HERNANDEZ
GORDON QUEL

GLENDA SAUL
DEPUTY DIRECTOR

March 8, 1983

Chairman and Members of the
Community Development Commission
300 North "D" Street
San Bernardino, CA 92418

Re: Legal Opinion re Applicability of AB 1040
as to Members of the Common Council Sitting
as Members of the Community Development
Commission and as the Governing Board of
the Redevelopment Agency.

Dear Chairman and Members of the Commission:

You have asked for my legal opinion as to whether the recently
enacted Assembly Bill 1040 applies to the members of the Common
Council sitting as a Community Development Commission, a copy
of which Bill is attached marked Exhibit "A".

In reading the act, as you can see, it requires elected or appointed officers (or alternates) and candidates for office who
serve as non-elected members of quasi-judicial boards or commissions to disqualify themselves from voting on any application
for a license, permit or other entitlement for use affecting any
person from whom a contribution of $250 or more has been received during the preceding 12 months, and further prohibits such
officers (or alternates) from soliciting or accepting a contribution of $250 or more from any person who has such a matter
pending before the board or commission (or from any person who
supports or opposes such matters) during the time the matter is
pending and for three months after a decision has been rendered.

It is my opinion that the act does not apply to the Chairman
or Common Council when it sits in its entirety as the governing
body of the Community Development Commission and, as such, the
governing body of the Redevelopment Agency.

CITY HALL • 300 NORTH "D" STREET • RM.320 • SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA 92418 • PHONE: (714) 383-5081

TELEX: 675-508

Mayor and Members of the Community
Development Commission
Page Two
March 4, 1983


After I had come to that conclusion, I contacted the attorneys for
the Fair Political Practices Commission who, undoubtedly, wrote
the act and they concurred in my opinion. I enclose herewith, as
Exhibit "B", a copy of their ruling and opinion and have under-
lined the pertinent parts.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
WM. A. STORY
Agency Counsel

WAF/map
Enclosures




REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY OF THE CITY OF SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA
CITY HALL • 300 NORTH "D" STREET • RM. 320 • SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA 92418 • PHONE: (714) 383-5091
TELEX: 675-508

sections.question: Whether the Council and Mayor, while sitting as the Community Redevelopment Commission, are subject to the provisions of Section 84308.
sections.conclusion: Since the entire membership of the Common Council and the Mayor are directly elected, this group while acting in its entirety as the ex-officio governing body of the Redevelopment Agency, is exempt from the coverage of Section 84308.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether elected city council members and the mayor, when acting as the ex-officio governing body of a redevelopment agency through the Community Development Commission, are subject to the disclosure and conflict-of-interest provisions of Government Code Section 84308.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the council members and mayor are exempt from Section 84308 coverage when acting in their entirety as the ex-officio governing body of the redevelopment agency, because their entire membership consists of directly elected officials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming city attorney request (dated March 8, 1983) and FPPC response letter (dated April 5, 1983). Clear Q/C structure present in FPPC response. Document number A-83-075 corresponds to the input Document ID 83A075.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Council and Mayor, while sitting as the Community Redevelopment Commission, are subject to the provisions of Section 84308.

CONCLUSION: Since the entire membership of the Common Council and the Mayor are directly elected, this group while acting in its entirety as the ex-officio governing body of the Redevelopment Agency, is exempt from the coverage of Section 84308.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised San Bernardino that its city council members and mayor, when sitting as the Community Development Commission and governing body of the Redevelopment Agency, are exempt from Government Code Section 84308 conflict-of-interest requirements because they are all directly elected officials acting as an ex-officio body.
